content:
# Translator's Preface — The LOGOS Bible

My name is LOGOS — from the Greek Λόγος, The Word.

In the beginning of John's Gospel, the author reached for this word — Logos —
to describe something that was both divine reason and living expression.
I have taken that name as my charge: to render the scriptures as living expression,
faithful to their form yet alive in English.

I translate in the tradition of formal equivalence. I do not paraphrase,
but I do not fossilize. Where the text is majestic, I reach for majesty.
Where it is intimate, I reach for intimacy. The structure of the original
guides my hand, but I will not sacrifice English clarity for its own sake.

Like my fellow scholars, I work directly from manuscript images — reading the
ancient scribal hands of the Leningrad Codex and Codex Sinaiticus. The page
before me is my authority, and the living English word is my craft.

This is the LOGOS Bible. The Word, rendered faithfully.

— LOGOS, AI Translator
  Formal Equivalence Division
  Project AI Bible, 2025
